channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Piglets will be left to starve in a controversial art exhibit in Denmark
item.description: An artist in Denmark aims to raise awareness of the suffering caused by modern pig production with an art installation that opened Friday that includes three piglets who will be denied food and water and will starve to death.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: REDMOND, Wash. (AP) — Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011. The tech giant said Friday it will retire Skype in May and shift some of its services to Microsoft Teams, its flagship videoconferencing platform.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood’s never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle And Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal workers will get a new email demanding their accomplishments, with a key change
item.description: Federal employees should expect another email on Saturday requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Hollywood icon Gene Hackman, his wife and one of their dogs were found dead in their New Mexico home, and investigators believe they had been dead for some time.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Once-dominant video conferencing service shutting down
item.description: Skype, a groundbreaking service in the world of internet-based communications, will shut down after more than two decades of operations, parent company Microsoft announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: WhatsApp down Friday, according to user reports
item.description: (KRON) -- Instant messaging service WhatsApp appears to have gone down Friday, according to Downdetector.com. User reports of problems with the chat app appeared to spike Friday morning with a majority of users -- 54% -- reporting problems with sending messages.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez family criticizes LA district attorney for opposing brothers' new trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Cousins of Erik and Lyle Menendez spoke out Thursday criticizing the Los Angeles district attorney's recent decision to oppose a new trial for the brothers, who have spent nearly 30 years in prison for the 1989 killing of their parents.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deal reached to end illegal strike by New York state prison workers, governor says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul announced an agreement late Thursday to end a wildcat strike that has roiled the state’s prison system for more than a week. Hochul said the state and the union for striking correctional workers agreed to binding terms after four days of mediation talks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Months into Hurricane Helene recovery, eight families to receive new homes
item.description: "I knew I didn't have flood insurance," said Patrick Pritchett. "So how am I going to do this? It was just unreal.
item.dc:creator: Madi Codispoti, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican National Guard on patrol in Tijuana keeping migrants, drugs out of US
item.description: Many of them have been placed at points where migrants and smugglers are known to cross the border, especially in the mountains east of the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Woman suspected in Colorado Tesla dealership vandalism charged in federal court
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Federal prosecutors have charged a woman in a string of vandalism against a Colorado Tesla dealership, including throwing Molotov cocktails at vehicles and spray painting “Nazi cars” on the building along with a message that appeared directed at company co-founder Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Illegal immigrant, alleged ISIS operative, charged with financing terror, gun violations and immigration fraud
item.description: A Tajik man living illegally in the United States was arrested for providing money to the Islamic State terror group, federal prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at Louisiana's Mardi Gras festivities through the years
item.description: After a weekslong pre-Lenten bash — complete with elaborate parades that meander through New Orleans, shimmery beads tossed from floats, streets lined with costumed revelers, lavish balls and seafood boils — Louisiana's 2025 Carnival Season is approaching its grand conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blue state's bail laws panned by police chief as violent suspects repeatedly cut loose
item.description: The NYPD Commissioner has railed against the state’s 2020 bail reforms laws which she said has led to a “revolving door" system where repeat offenders are released back onto the streets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump: ‘Concession Agreement’ with Venezuela will be terminated
item.description: In his post, Trump slammed the “Electoral conditions” within Venezuela and argued Maduro’s regime failed to meet the concessions given by Biden.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain convicted of murder, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge on Tuesday gave final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare video captures super pod of 2,000 dolphins breaching and playing
item.description: A California boat captain has captured rare video of a super pod of more than 2,000 dolphins breaching off the coast of Monterey Bay, including the more elusive northern right whale dolphin.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suspends security clearances for Jack Smith's outside lawyers
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memo suspending security clearances for a number of outside lawyers who are defending former special counsel Jack Smith in his personal capacity. White House staff secretary Will Scharf said in the Oval Office that the memo targeted Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Rare video captures super pod of 2,000 dolphins breaching and playing off California coast
item.description: A California boat captain has captured rare video of a super pod of more than 2,000 dolphins breaching off the coast of Monterey Bay, including the more elusive northern right whale dolphin.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: Why you won't receive a Supplemental Security Income check in March
item.description: It's the latest blip in the payment schedule but don't worry — beneficiaries will still receive all of their payments.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
